---
title: "Source Guide: Orthogonality and Least Squares"
description: "A curated source guide for learning orthogonality and least squares from first pass through research bridge."
page-type: source-guide
status: published
update-status: current
level: foundation
topic: linear-algebra
prerequisites:
  - /topics/foundation/linear-algebra/concepts/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Choose the right resource for first exposure, second pass, or paper bridge."
  - "Separate durable references from current research-facing references."
  - "See which sources belong to the concept, application, and research pages in this package."
applications:
  - regression
  - numerical-linear-algebra
keywords:
  - source guide
  - least squares
  - orthogonality
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: semiannual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
stable-sources:
  - "MIT 18.06SC Linear Algebra resource index"
  - "Stanford Math 51"
  - "Hefferon, Linear Algebra"
current-sources:
  - "Acta Numerica RandNLA survey"
  - "JMLR 2023 benign overfitting"
  - "NeurIPS 2024 distributed least squares"
---

## How To Use This Guide

Use exactly one source from each layer at a time:

- `First pass` for intuition and basic fluency
- `Second pass` for proofs, exercises, and numerics
- `Paper bridge` for reading current research

Do not try to read everything in parallel.

## Stable Core

- [MIT 18.06SC Linear Algebra resource index](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-06sc-linear-algebra-fall-2011/pages/resource-index/) - `First pass` - best official structured path if you want lectures, problems, and projection-focused least-squares coverage. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Stanford Math 51](https://web.stanford.edu/class/math51/) - `First pass` - strong current course framing for applied linear algebra with regression and data-facing motivation. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Hefferon, Linear Algebra](https://hefferon.net/linearalgebra/) - `Second pass` - especially strong for motivated proofs and self-study exercises. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [MIT 2.086 Unit 3: Matrices and Least Squares; Regression from Math, Numerics, and Programming](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/2-086-numerical-computation-for-mechanical-engineers-fall-2012/resources/mit2_086f12_notes_unit3/) - `Second pass` - excellent when you want regression, numerics, and programming concerns in one place. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Current Bridge

- [Randomized Numerical Linear Algebra: Foundations and Algorithms](https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/acta-numerica/article/randomized-numerical-linear-algebra-foundations-and-algorithms/4486926746CFF4547F42A2996C7DC09C) - `Second pass` - modern survey context for how least squares fits into sketching and large-scale numerical methods. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [The Implicit Bias of Benign Overfitting](https://www.jmlr.org/papers/v24/22-0784.html) - `Paper bridge` - current perspective on minimum-norm interpolation and high-dimensional regression. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Benign Overfitting of Constant-Stepsize SGD for Linear Regression](https://jmlr.org/beta/papers/v24/21-1297.html) - `Paper bridge` - current view linking least squares to optimization dynamics. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Distributed Least Squares in Small Space via Sketching and Bias Reduction](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2024/hash/86c283920335ed1fec3edee227e05fbf-Abstract-Conference.html) - `Paper bridge` - useful current example when the systems side matters. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Paper Bridge

- [A Statistical Perspective on Randomized Sketching for Ordinary Least-Squares](https://jmlr.org/papers/v17/15-440.html) - `Paper bridge` - best first bridge paper for this topic package because it keeps the core least-squares geometry visible. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Iterative Hessian Sketch: Fast and Accurate Solution Approximation for Constrained Least-Squares](https://jmlr.org/beta/papers/v17/14-460.html) - `Paper bridge` - stronger optimization-facing follow-up once the first sketching paper is comfortable. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Recommended Reading Paths

### Path 1: First serious understanding

1. MIT 18.06SC resource index
2. [Orthogonality and Least Squares](../concepts/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd)
3. Hefferon for more exercises

### Path 2: Regression and computation

1. [Orthogonality and Least Squares](../concepts/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd)
2. MIT 2.086 Unit 3 notes
3. [Computation Lab: Projection Geometry and Regression Residuals](../labs/orthogonality-and-least-squares-geometry.qmd)
4. [Linear Regression Through Projection](../applications/linear-regression-through-projection.qmd)

### Path 3: Bridge to research

1. [Orthogonality and Least Squares](../concepts/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd)
2. [Projection Theorem and Normal Equations](../proofs/projection-theorem-and-normal-equations.qmd)
3. [Paper Lab: Randomized Sketching for Least Squares](../paper-lab/randomized-sketching-for-least-squares.qmd)
4. [Research Direction: Least Squares, Sketching, and Overparameterized Regression](../research/least-squares-sketching-and-overparameterized-regression.qmd)
5. Acta Numerica survey or current JMLR paper

## Sources Checked Online

All links above were checked on `2026-04-24`.

The online verification pass included:

- official course pages
- official author-maintained textbook pages
- official journal or proceedings pages
- official conference proceedings page for the 2024 NeurIPS example
